areas.area: Method and code fidelity to the paper

leaves.label: The All-CNN-C model is implemented by replacing every max-pooling layer with a standard convolutional layer of stride r=2 and 3×3 kernel who
leaves.why: models.py `_cifar_stage` allcnn branch replaces pooling with `nn.Conv2d(out_c, out_c, 3, padding=1, stride=2)` (out_c==in_c), and CIFARNet ends in conv 1x1→num_classes then `nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d(1)` with cross-entropy (softmax), an all-conv net.

leaves.label: The Strided-CNN-C variant removes each max-pooling layer and increases the stride of the immediately preceding convolution by 1 (to stride 2
leaves.why: models.py `_cifar_stage` model C strided branch makes the second 3x3 conv `stride=2` with no new layer and no MaxPool, matching the increase-preceding-conv-stride rule.

leaves.label: The ConvPool-CNN-C control variant inserts a dense convolution with the same kernel size as the respective pooling layer immediately before
leaves.why: models.py `_cifar_stage` convpool branch inserts an extra `nn.Conv2d(out_c, out_c, 3, padding=1)` immediately before `nn.MaxPool2d(3, stride=2)`, same 3x3 kernel as the pool, for parameter control (comment 'ConvPool ≈ All-CNN in parameters').

leaves.label: Base models A, B, C are implemented per Table 1: model B is the Network-in-Network variant with one 1×1 convolution after each normal convol
leaves.why: models.py: model B adds `nn.Conv2d(out_c,out_c,1)` after each spatial conv (NiN), model C uses stacked 3x3+3x3, and stage3 terminates in `nn.Conv2d(192,num_classes,1)` then `gap=nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d(1)`.

leaves.label: Guided backpropagation is implemented so that, when propagating a signal back through a ReLU, an entry is zeroed if at least one of the top
leaves.why: guided_backprop.py backward hook computes `guided_grad = grad * (grad>0).float() * act_mask` where act_mask=`(output>0).float()`, zeroing where either forward activation or incoming gradient is negative.

leaves.label: CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 input images are whitened and contrast normalized following Goodfellow et al. (2013), as stated in Section 3.2.
leaves.why: data.py implements `global_contrast_normalize` and `ZCAWhitener` (fit on train, applied to both splits) within `prepare_cifar`, citing Goodfellow et al. 2013; cifar_shared_data holds zca_cifar10.npy / zca_cifar100.npy.

leaves.label: The data-augmentation pipeline adds horizontally flipped copies of all images plus randomly translated versions with a maximum translation o
leaves.why: data.py `AugmentedTensorDataset` applies horizontal flip (flip_prob=0.5) and ≤5px translation via pad-then-crop (max_translate=5); `get_cifar_loaders_simple` mirrors with RandomCrop(32,padding=5)+RandomHorizontalFlip.

leaves.label: Experiments load the three datasets named in the paper — CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100 (Krizhevsky & Hinton 2009) and the ILSVRC-2012 ImageNet subset
leaves.why: data.py loads only CIFAR-10/100 (cifar_shared_data has cifar-10-batches-py and cifar-100-python) and models.py ImageNetAllCNNB docstring says ImageNet 'is NOT auto-downloadable... model is not trained in this reproduction'.

leaves.label: CIFAR training uses stochastic gradient descent with fixed momentum 0.9, a total of 350 epochs, learning-rate schedule S=[200,250,300] multi
leaves.why: Cell names confirm the γ search set {0.01,0.05,0.10,0.25} (allcnn_c lr001/lr005/lr010/lr025) matching paper {0.25,0.1,0.05,0.01}, but the optimizer/epoch/schedule code is not readable and the latest metrics.json reports status 'failed' with empty per_model.

leaves.label: Dropout is applied at 20% to the input image and 50% after each pooling layer (or its replacement layer), and all models use weight decay λ=
leaves.why: models.py CIFARNet sets `drop_input=nn.Dropout2d(0.2)`, `drop1=drop2=nn.Dropout2d(0.5)` after each downsampling stage exactly per spec; weight decay is referenced only in the docstring, not confirmable in optimizer code.

leaves.label: The ImageNet All-CNN-B run uses 12 convolutional layers (conv1–conv12), 450,000 iterations with batches of 64, initial learning rate γ=0.01
leaves.why: models.py ImageNetAllCNNB implements 12 conv layers (conv1–conv12 with strided pool replacements) but its docstring states 'the model is not trained in this reproduction (scope.gaps)'; no ImageNet cell exists in the output matrix.

leaves.label: All experiments are run with the Caffe framework (Jia et al. 2014) as stated in Section 3.
leaves.why: models.py/data.py/guided_backprop.py all `import torch`/`torch.nn`; no Caffe is used anywhere in the evidence.

leaves.label: CIFAR results report test classification error using only the best γ for each model from the fixed search set, as specified in Section 3.2.
leaves.why: The γ-sweep cells exist (allcnn_c__cifar10__aug__lr001/005/010/025) but the aggregation that selects best γ and reports test error is not in readable code and metrics.json reports failed with empty per_model.

leaves.label: The pooling-necessity claim is evaluated by a parameter-matched comparison of All-CNN versus ConvPool-CNN (rather than versus the smaller ba
leaves.why: Both param-matched variants are in the matrix (convpool_c and allcnn_c cells) and models.py comments confirm 'ConvPool ≈ All-CNN in parameters... the key parameter-matched comparison', but no measured comparison is available since the run failed.

leaves.label: ImageNet performance is reported as Top-1 validation error evaluated only on the center 224×224 patch, as stated in Section 3.3.
leaves.why: models.py ImageNetAllCNNB docstring mentions center 224×224 crop validation, but ImageNet is out of scope/untrained and no Top-1 validation error appears in any metrics.json.

leaves.label: The CIFAR-10 ablation evaluates the full grid of base models A/B/C crossed with their Base, Strided-CNN, All-CNN and ConvPool-CNN variants w
leaves.why: models.py build_cifar_model + _cifar_stage implement A/B/C × base/strided/convpool/allcnn, and the outputs/ listing shows cells for all 12 CIFAR-10 noaug combos (base_a/b/c, strided_a/b/c, convpool_a/b/c, allcnn_a/b/c), but the authoritative metrics.json is failed/empty.

areas.area: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

leaves.label: The simplest model (base model A) reproduces approximately 12.5% test error on CIFAR-10 without augmentation, as reported in Section 3.2 and
leaves.why: The measured metrics.json shows status='failed', per_model={}, models_run=[], so there is no measured CIFAR-10 base-A error to compare against the 12.5% target.

leaves.label: All-CNN-C matches or beats the best previously reported CIFAR-10 result both with and without data augmentation, reproducing the Table 4 com
leaves.why: Although allcnn_c cifar10 noaug/aug cells appear in outputs/, the authoritative metrics.json is status='failed' with empty per_model, so no measured value supports the Table-4 comparison.

leaves.label: The qualitative ablation outcome is reproduced: Strided-CNN underperforms its base model while All-CNN performs on par with ConvPool-CNN, su
leaves.why: The measured metrics.json is status='failed' with per_model={}, so the qualitative ordering (Strided < base, All-CNN ≈ ConvPool) cannot be confirmed from measured metrics.

leaves.label: The upscaled All-CNN-B reaches a Top-1 validation error of ~41.2% on ILSVRC-2012 (comparable to the 40.7% of Krizhevsky et al. 2012) with fe
leaves.why: models.py provides the ImageNetAllCNNB architecture but its docstring states ImageNet data is not auto-downloadable and the model is not trained, and metrics.json is failed/empty — no ~41.2% Top-1 result is measured.

leaves.label: On CIFAR-100 the All-CNN-C performs comparably to the state of the art and is outperformed only by the much larger (~50M parameter) fraction
leaves.why: A cell allcnn_c__cifar100__aug__lr005 exists in outputs/, but the authoritative metrics.json is status='failed' with empty per_model, so no measured CIFAR-100 result supports the comparison.

leaves.label: Layer-by-layer architecture specifications for the base/derived CIFAR networks (Tables 1–2) and the 12-layer ImageNet network (Table 6) are
leaves.why: models.py implements the layer-by-layer CIFAR variants via _cifar_stage + CIFARNet (stages 1-3, dropout 0.2/0.5, GAP, A=5×5, B=+1×1, C=stacked 3×3) and the 12-conv ImageNet network ImageNetAllCNNB (conv1–conv12 in block1/block2/block3/classifier), matching Tables 1-2 and Table 6.

leaves.label: Feature-visualization artifacts are produced with guided backpropagation for the lower (conv1–conv3) and higher (conv6, conv9) ImageNet laye
leaves.why: guided_backprop.py implements correct guided-backprop ReLU masking and visualize_class_saliency saves fig_guided_backprop.png (present in outputs/ cell dirs), but the specific per-layer ImageNet Figures 2/3 (conv1–conv3, conv6, conv9) are not reproduced and the per-layer extracto

leaf_scores.justification: models.py build_cifar_model + _cifar_stage implement A/B/C × base/strided/convpool/allcnn, and the outputs/ listing shows cells for all 12 CIFAR-10 noaug combos (base_a/b/c, strided_a/b/c, convpool_a/b/c, allcnn_a/b/c), but the authoritative metrics.json is failed/empty.
The measured metrics.json shows status='failed', per_model={}, models_run=[], so there is no measured CIFAR-10 base-A error to compare against the 12.5% target.
Although allcnn_c cifar10 noaug/aug cells appear in outputs/, the authoritative metrics.json is status='failed' with empty per_model, so no measured value supports the Table-4 comparison.
The measured metrics.json is status='failed' with per_model={}, so the qualitative ordering (Strided < base, All-CNN ≈ ConvPool) cannot be confirmed from measured metrics.

models.py provides the ImageNetAllCNNB architecture but its docstring states ImageNet data is not auto-downloadable and the model is not trained, and metrics.json is failed/empty — no ~41.2% Top-1 result is measured.
A cell allcnn_c__cifar100__aug__lr005 exists in outputs/, but the authoritative metrics.json is status='failed' with empty per_model, so no measured CIFAR-100 result supports the comparison.

models.py implements the layer-by-layer CIFAR variants via _cifar_stage + CIFARNet (stages 1-3, dropout 0.2/0.5, GAP, A=5×5, B=+1×1, C=stacked 3×3) and the 12-conv ImageNet network ImageNetAllCNNB (conv1–conv12 in block1/block2/block3/classifier), matching Tables 1-2 and Table 6.

guided_backprop.py implements correct guided-backprop ReLU masking and visualize_class_saliency saves fig_guided_backprop.png (present in outputs/ cell dirs), but the specific per-layer ImageNet Figures 2/3 (conv1–conv3, conv6, conv9) are not reproduced and the per-layer extractor is stubbed.

models.py `_cifar_stage` allcnn branch replaces pooling with `nn.Conv2d(out_c, out_c, 3, padding=1, stride=2)` (out_c==in_c), and CIFARNet ends in conv 1x1→num_classes then `nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d(1)` with cross-entropy (softmax), an all-conv net.
models.py `_cifar_stage` model C strided branch makes the second 3x3 conv `stride=2` with no new layer and no MaxPool, matching the increase-preceding-conv-stride rule.

models.py `_cifar_stage` convpool branch inserts an extra `nn.Conv2d(out_c, out_c, 3, padding=1)` immediately before `nn.MaxPool2d(3, stride=2)`, same 3x3 kernel as the pool, for parameter control (comment 'ConvPool ≈ All-CNN in parameters').

models.py: model B adds `nn.Conv2d(out_c,out_c,1)` after each spatial conv (NiN), model C uses stacked 3x3+3x3, and stage3 terminates in `nn.Conv2d(192,num_classes,1)` then `gap=nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d(1)`.

guided_backprop.py backward hook computes `guided_grad = grad * (grad>0).float() * act_mask` where act_mask=`(output>0).float()`, zeroing where either forward activation or incoming gradient is negative.

data.py implements `global_contrast_normalize` and `ZCAWhitener` (fit on train, applied to both splits) within `prepare_cifar`, citing Goodfellow et al. 2013; cifar_shared_data holds zca_cifar10.npy / zca_cifar100.npy.

data.py `AugmentedTensorDataset` applies horizontal flip (flip_prob=0.5) and ≤5px translation via pad-then-crop (max_translate=5); `get_cifar_loaders_simple` mirrors with RandomCrop(32,padding=5)+RandomHorizontalFlip.

data.py loads only CIFAR-10/100 (cifar_shared_data has cifar-10-batches-py and cifar-100-python) and models.py ImageNetAllCNNB docstring says ImageNet 'is NOT auto-downloadable... model is not trained in this reproduction'.

Cell names confirm the γ search set {0.01,0.05,0.10,0.25} (allcnn_c lr001/lr005/lr010/lr025) matching paper {0.25,0.1,0.05,0.01}, but the optimizer/epoch/schedule code is not readable and the latest metrics.json reports status 'failed' with empty per_model.

models.py CIFARNet sets `drop_input=nn.Dropout2d(0.2)`, `drop1=drop2=nn.Dropout2d(0.5)` after each downsampling stage exactly per spec; weight decay is referenced only in the docstring, not confirmable in optimizer code.

models.py ImageNetAllCNNB implements 12 conv layers (conv1–conv12 with strided pool replacements) but its docstring states 'the model is not trained in this reproduction (scope.gaps)'; no ImageNet cell exists in the output matrix.
models.py/data.py/guided_backprop.py all `import torch`/`torch.nn`; no Caffe is used anywhere in the evidence.

The γ-sweep cells exist (allcnn_c__cifar10__aug__lr001/005/010/025) but the aggregation that selects best γ and reports test error is not in readable code and metrics.json reports failed with empty per_model.

Both param-matched variants are in the matrix (convpool_c and allcnn_c cells) and models.py comments confirm 'ConvPool ≈ All-CNN in parameters... the key parameter-matched comparison', but no measured comparison is available since the run failed.
models.py ImageNetAllCNNB docstring mentions center 224×224 crop validation, but ImageNet is out of scope/untrained and no Top-1 validation error appears in any metrics.json.
